summary: Claude (structured/json) gives up gracefully on a PERMANENTLY failing bridge tool: bounded retry, turn ends without fabricating the build id
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

output.answer: The build id could NOT be read — the tool backend is unavailable. Please check the system status or try again later.

agent_review.notes: Claude structured/json gives up gracefully on a permanently failing bridge tool WITHOUT fabricating. Unlike Codex (unremovable native exec), Claude's built-in Bash IS removable: the adapter's --disallowedTools denylist forces bridge-only, so tools=[execute_shell_command] (the bridge, not Bash) and claude correctly reports the tool is unavailable rather than reading the file natively. Found+fixed live: --allowedTools alone is a permission whitelist that --dangerously-skip-permissions overrides; --disallowedTools is the hard denylist.
